skill.name: stitch-ui-design
skill.description: Guides users in writing precise Google Stitch prompts for UI design, app screens, responsive layouts, design systems, and design-to-code handoff.
skill.summary: Create stronger Google Stitch prompts for web and mobile UI design.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found markdown design prompts and UI terminology only. No executable code, network calls, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to fenced prompt examples and workflow text. These are documentation blocks for Google Stitch prompts, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown code fences containing natural language UI prompts. No executable files, shell invocations, or command interpreters are present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Design Terms Flagged as Security Patterns
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts match ordinary words in design documentation, such as design, desktop, dashboard, password fields, and prompt examples. They do not describe cryptographic code or host inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is UI guidance and sample screen content. No hashing, encryption, system probing, or environment collection behavior appears in these locations.

content.user_title: Create Better Google Stitch UI Prompts
content.value_statement: Google Stitch can produce generic designs when prompts lack detail. This skill gives structured prompt patterns for screens, flows, visual style, accessibility, and handoff.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to structure Google Stitch prompts with context, features, style, and platform requirements.
Provides examples for landing pages, mobile apps, dashboards, e-commerce, authentication, content platforms, and SaaS tools.
Guides iterative refinement with annotations, variants, and progressive prompt updates.
Covers responsive design, accessibility requirements, reusable components, and state-based UI prompts.
Describes export and handoff workflows for HTML, CSS, Figma, and developer-ready specifications.
limitations: Does not call Google Stitch or generate designs directly inside the assistant.
Does not validate final visual quality, accessibility, or production code automatically.
Relies on the user to provide product context, brand details, and platform constraints.
Google Stitch features may change because the tool is experimental.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Product Interface
use_cases.description: Create a detailed Stitch prompt for a landing page, dashboard, or app flow before design review.

use_cases.title: Generate Design System Screens
use_cases.description: Define colors, typography, spacing, components, states, and responsive behavior for consistent UI output.

use_cases.title: Prepare Design Handoff
use_cases.description: Refine Stitch exports with accessibility, semantic structure, asset notes, and developer-ready specifications.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Screen Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for one clear screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me write a Google Stitch prompt for a [screen type] for [target user]. Include key sections, visual style, and platform.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Screen App Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Stitch prompt for a [product type] with onboarding, home, detail, and settings screens. Include navigation and state requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Design System Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for reusable interface patterns.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a Stitch prompt that defines design tokens, core components, responsive rules, accessibility targets, and interaction states for [brand].

prompt_templates.title: Production Handoff Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Stitch design goal and create a handoff-focused prompt with semantic structure, assets, breakpoints, states, and developer notes.

output_examples.input: I need a mobile food delivery home screen.
output_examples.output: A structured Stitch prompt with search, categories, restaurant cards, bottom navigation, visual style, and mobile sizing requirements.

output_examples.input: Make my SaaS dashboard prompt more specific.
output_examples.output: A refined prompt that names metrics, chart types, navigation, empty states, loading states, responsive layout, and accessibility checks.

output_examples.input: Prepare a design for handoff after Stitch export.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering semantic HTML, component names, color tokens, image assets, responsive breakpoints, ARIA labels, and Figma organization.

best_practices: Include the user context, screen purpose, platform, layout, components, and visual style in every prompt.
Ask for states such as loading, empty, error, selected, disabled, and success before exporting.
Refine designs in small steps with annotations instead of replacing the full prompt each time.
anti_patterns: Avoid vague requests such as asking for a nice website without audience, content, or layout details.
Do not omit responsive behavior when the design must work across mobile, tablet, and desktop.
Do not treat exported HTML or CSS as production-ready without accessibility and code review.

faq.question: Does this skill use Google Stitch directly?
faq.answer: No. It helps write better prompts for Google Stitch, but it does not call the Stitch service.

faq.question: Can it help with mobile and web UI?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes prompt patterns for mobile apps, responsive web pages, dashboards, forms, and SaaS interfaces.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for prompt drafting and review.

faq.question: Does it cover accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends prompt details for contrast, touch targets, keyboard navigation, labels, and screen reader support.

faq.question: Will it make production-ready code?
faq.answer: No. It can guide export cleanup, but developers should review generated HTML, CSS, and assets before production use.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the product goal, target users, screen list, brand style, platform, key components, and required interaction states.
